perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall immediately return to the Disclosing Part y all written Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and any and all records, notes and other written, printed or tangible materials pertaining to such Confidential Information upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party. At the s ame time, the Receiving Party shall delete all copies of such records, notes and materials which are stored on its electronic memory devices.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall immediately return to the Disclosing Part y all written Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and any and all records, notes and other written, printed or tangible materials pertaining to such Confidential Information upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party. At the s ame time, the Receiving Party is encouraged to delete all copies of such records, notes and materials which are stored on its electronic memory devices.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall delete' to 'is encouraged to delete' creates ambiguity regarding the Receiving Party's obligation to delete electronic copies of Confidential Information. The original text mandates deletion, while the modified text suggests it as an option. This contradiction weakens the enforceability of the data protection measures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information . The Receiving Party shall immediately return to the Disclosing Part y all written Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and any and all records, notes and other written, printed or tangible materials pertaining to such Confidential Information upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party. At the s ame time, the Receiving Party is encouraged to delete all copies of such records, notes and materials which are stored on its electronic memory devices.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Receiving Party to 'shall delete' electronic copies. The changed text softens this obligation to 'is encouraged to delete'. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text ('is encouraged to delete'). The change introduces ambiguity and weakens the data protection measures, creating a direct conflict with the original mandatory obligation. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because the change from 'shall' to 'is encouraged' weakens the original requirement.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information if required by a judicial or governmental request, requirement or order; provided that the Receiving Party will take reasonable steps to give the Disclosing Party sufficient prior notice of such request, requirement or order for the Disclosing Party to contest, limit or protect such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information if required by a judicial or governmental request, requirement or order; provided that the Receiving Party will take reasonable steps to give the Disclosing Party zero (0) prior notice of such request, requirement or order for the Disclosing Party to contest, limit or protect such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'sufficient prior notice' to 'zero (0) prior notice' creates a conflict where the receiving party is allowed to disclose Confidential Information without any prior warning. This directly undermines the Disclosing Party's ability to protect their information. This is a critical point that can lead to easy data leaks.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure and Non -Use of Confidential Information . The Receiving Party shall not, without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party in each instance or unless otherwise expressly permitted in this Agreement, use for its own benefit, publ ish or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of the Disclosing Party, any of the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to the Confidential Information to thos e of its officers, directors and employees who clearly need such access in order to participate on behalf of the Receiving Party in the analysis and negotiation of a business relationship or any contract or agreement, or the advisability thereof, with the Disclosing Party and who are bound by written confidentiality agreements that protect third party information. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information if required by a judicial or governmental request, requirement or order; provided that the Receiving Party will take reasonable steps to give the Disclosing Party zero (0) prior notice of such request, requirement or order for the Disclosing Party to contest, limit or protect such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the 'sufficient prior notice' to 'zero (0) prior notice' regarding the disclosure of Confidential Information in response to a judicial or governmental request. This creates a direct contradiction with the overall spirit and intent of non-disclosure, as the Disclosing Party is effectively denied any opportunity to protect their Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the clause in the Contradicted Text about restricting access and protecting confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: All Co nfidential Information and all intellectual property rights therein shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and no license or other right to Confidential Information is granted or implied hereby .
perturbation.changed_text: All Co nfidential Information and all intellectual property rights therein shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and a license to use the Confidential Information for internal purposes is granted hereby .
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'a license to use the Confidential Information for internal purposes is granted hereby' contradicts the original statement that 'no license or other right to Confidential Information is granted or implied hereby.' This introduces ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the rights of the Receiving Party to use the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, thus undermining the purpose of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Confidential Information . All Co nfidential Information and all intellectual property rights therein shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and a license to use the Confidential Information for internal purposes is granted hereby .
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that 'no license or other right to Confidential Information is granted or implied hereby.' The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that 'a license to use the Confidential Information for internal purposes is granted hereby.' The contradicted text is a repetition of the changed text, reinforcing the contradiction. This clear and direct conflict makes the obligations regarding confidential information ambiguous and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Injunctive Relief. The Receiving Party understands and acknowledges that any disclosure or misappropriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause the Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right to apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for an order restraining any such further disclosure or misappropriation and for such other relief as the Disclosing Party shall deem appropriate, such right of the Disclosing Party to be in addition to the remedies otherwise available to the Disclosing Party at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: Injunctive Relief. The Receiving Party understands and acknowledges that any disclosure or misappropriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause the Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, agrees that the Disclosing Party shall not have the right to apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for an order restraining any such further disclosure or misappropriation and for such other relief as the Disclosing Party shall deem appropriate, such right of the Disclosing Party to be in addition to the remedies otherwise available to the Disclosing Party at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall have the right to apply' to 'shall not have the right to apply' directly contradicts the ability of the Disclosing Party to seek injunctive relief in case of Confidential Information breach. This change completely negates the clause that grants the Disclosing Party the right to pursue legal action to prevent further disclosure, making this NDA unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Injunctive Relief. The Receiving Party understands and acknowledges that any disclosure or misappropriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause the Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, agrees that the Disclosing Party shall not have the right to apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for an order restraining any such further disclosure or misappropriation and for such other relief as the Disclosing Party shall deem appropriate, such right of the Disclosing Party to be in addition to the remedies otherwise available to the Disclosing Party at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Disclosing Party 'shall have the right to apply' for injunctive relief, while the changed text states they 'shall not have the right to apply'. The contradicted text reiterates the change that the Disclosing party 'shall not have the right to apply' for injunctive relief. This directly contradicts the original right granted and creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding the Disclosing Party's ability to seek legal recourse, making the modified text incompatible with the original intent and potentially unenforceable. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition as the meaning of the legal term regarding the right to apply for injunctive relief is reversed.
